title: Uganda Medical Preparation (DEMO - Historical)
description: Healthcare preparation during expulsion. Hospitals were understaffed and dangerous. Medical infrastructure collapsed during Amin years.
items: Stock essential medications - pharmacies had shortages. International supply chains disrupted.
Prescriptions and medical records - translated to English if necessary for destination country doctors
Vaccination documentation - yellow fever, cholera, smallpox certificates critical for international travel
Chronic conditions - ensure sufficient supply for several months. Refills in destination country would take time.
Malaria prophylaxis - for journey and initial period in transit
First aid supplies - for immediate needs and minor emergencies
Children's medications - fever reducers, antibiotics if available, rehydration salts
Eyeglasses and prescriptions - replacements would be expensive and time-consuming in destination country
Dental work - complete urgent dental treatment before leaving if possible. Destination country care expensive.
Medical conditions requiring ongoing care - dialysis, insulin, heart conditions particularly challenging during displacement
Traditional medicines - some brought Ayurvedic or herbal preparations not available in UK/Canada
Prenatal care - pregnant women faced dangerous conditions during evacuation and in camps
Mental health - no formal preparation but trauma was universal. Community and faith provided coping mechanisms.
